definition: The act of fleeing or hiding from supervision, typically by failing to report to a probation officer and making oneself unavailable. There is no statutory definition, and courts disagree on exactly what conduct qualifies — some require only a failure to appear, while others require a deliberate pattern of conduct that makes supervision impossible.
examples: Rico absconded from her supervised release by making herself unavailable to her probation officer for an extended period, which triggered an arrest warrant.
